title: ISON-Guideline - Standards of Care in Neuropelveology
author: possover, marc
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
ISON-Guideline – Standards of Care in Neuropelveology  Version 1 – June 2026 Developed under the auspices of the International Society of Neuropelveology (ISON) 
 This guideline presents the first comprehensive international standards of care dedicated to Neuropelveology, an emerging interdisciplinary field focused on the diagnosis and treatment of disorders involving the pelvic nervous system. The document defines the conceptual foundations, diagnostic principles, surgical standards, neurovascular safety requirements, training pathways, ethical responsibilities, and institutional criteria necessary for safe neuropelveological practice. 
 The guideline emphasizes that neuropelveology cannot be reduced to a surgical technique or minimally invasive access route alone, but requires advanced understanding of pelvic neuroanatomy, neurophysiology, neurological diagnosis, retroperitoneal neurovascular surgery, and long-term functional patient management. Particular attention is given to the prevention of irreversible neurological injury, vascular complications, chronic neuropathic pain, and functional pelvic disorders. 
 Topics covered include: 
 
 diagnostic responsibility and preoperative evaluation, 
 principles of nerve preservation, 
 thermal and neurovascular surgical safety, 
 pelvic nerve tumors and oncological principles, 
 vascular entrapment surgery, 
 intraoperative neurofunctional monitoring, 
 postoperative rehabilitation and prevention of central sensitization, 
 structured certification pathways and institutional accreditation, 
 ethical and medico-legal aspects of pelvic nerve surgery. 
 
 The guideline further proposes international standards for training and certification in neuropelveology and recommends that independent use of the professional designation “Neuropelveologist” should correspond to advanced structured training and documented competence (ISON Level 3 Certification). 
 This document is intended for surgeons, neurologists, neurosurgeons, gynecologists, urologists, colorectal surgeons, pain specialists, rehabilitation physicians, researchers, and healthcare institutions involved in the management of pelvic nerve disorders. 
 Keywords: Neuropelveology, pelvic nerves, pelvic neuropathic pain, sacral plexus, sciatic nerve, pudendal nerve, retroperitoneal surgery, neurovascular surgery, pelvic nerve entrapment, endometriosis, neuromodulation, pelvic schwannoma, surgical guidelines, nerve preservation, robotic surgery, LION procedure, GNS, ISON.
